title: How an Interpreter Works
description: Interpreters read, translate, and execute code line-by-line in a continuous loop at runtime.
codeBlock:
Source Code Line
  ↓
Read Line
  ↓
Parse Statement
  ↓
Execute Instructions (Stack/Memory)
  ↓
Output / Error (Halt on first error)
details: An interpreter reads statements and runs them immediately without producing an intermediate binary. For a detailed step-by-step visual run, view the Interactive Interpreter Guide.
